element.name: Monstrous Compendium Volume One: Spelljammer Creatures

set.description: Wizards of the Coast, D&D Beyond
set.name: author

element.name: Ziklight
element.source: Monstrous Compendium Volume One: Spelljammer Creatures

element.description:
A puppeteer parasite looks like a rubbery amoeba the size of a dinner plate. Its dorsal surface is soft and glossy, while its ventral surface is lined with bony hooks. The parasite uses its hooks to attach to a wall or ceiling until suitable prey passes nearby.

A parasite that comes in physical contact with a Humanoid uses its hooks to latch onto it. The parasite can then drain life energy from that creature or use it as transportation. The parasite can also impose its will on a nearby creature, forcing the creature to comply with its wishes. Puppeteer parasites like to use Humanoid thralls as bodyguards and transports.
element.name: Puppeteer Parasite

set.description: blindsight 60 ft. (blind beyond this distance), passive Perception 12
set.name: senses

element.source: Monstrous Compendium Volume One: Spelljammer Creatures

element.description: The parasite doesn't require air or sleep.
element.name: Unusual Nature
element.sheet: The parasite doesn't require air or sleep.
element.source: Monstrous Compendium Volume One: Spelljammer Creatures

element.description: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) necrotic damage, and the parasite attaches to the target. While attached, the parasite can't make Cling attacks. The parasite can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. As an action, a creature within reach of the parasite can try to detach it, doing so with a successful DC 14 Strength check.
element.name: Cling
element.sheet: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) necrotic damage, and the parasite attaches to the target. While attached, the parasite can't make Cling attacks. The parasite can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. As an action, a creature within reach of the parasite can try to detach it, doing so with a successful DC 14 Strength check.
element.source: Monstrous Compendium Volume One: Spelljammer Creatures

element.description: The parasite deals 12 (3d6 + 2) necrotic damage to one creature it is physically attached to, provided that creature isn't a Construct or an Undead. The parasite regains hit points equal to the damage taken.
element.name: Consume Life
element.sheet: The parasite deals 12 (3d6 + 2) necrotic damage to one creature it is physically attached to, provided that creature isn't a Construct or an Undead. The parasite regains hit points equal to the damage taken.
element.source: Monstrous Compendium Volume One: Spelljammer Creatures

element.description: The parasite casts the suggestion spell, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13).
element.name: Suggestion (Psionics; 1/Day, Bonus Action)

sheet.content: The parasite casts the suggestion spell, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13).
